title: quercus utahensis subsp. submollis
intro: Quercus utahensis subsp. submollis, also known as the Utah live oak, is a subspecies of the Utah live oak tree, belonging to the white oak family (Quercus spp.). It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The leaves and bark of the plant are said to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and supporting cardiovascular health. Quercus utahensis subsp. submollis can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions, which are traditionally used to promote overall well-being and alleviate various health conditions.
